faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers about NYT Strands and how our hints work.

faqItems.question: What is NYT Strands?
faqItems.answer: NYT Strands is a daily word puzzle by The New York Times. You are given a 6×8 grid of letters and must find theme words by connecting adjacent letters. Each puzzle has a theme clue, several theme words (highlighted in blue), and a special Spangram word (highlighted in gold) that spans the entire board and ties the theme together.

faqItems.question: How do the progressive hints work?
faqItems.answer: Each puzzle has up to five hint levels. Level 1 gives a vague thematic clue. Level 2 narrows it down. Level 3 reveals partial information about word lengths. Level 4 gives starting letters. Level 5 reveals the full answers. You choose how much help you want.

faqItems.question: When does the daily Strands puzzle reset?
faqItems.answer: A new NYT Strands puzzle is released every day at midnight Eastern Time (ET). Our hints are published shortly after.

faqItems.question: Is this site affiliated with The New York Times?
faqItems.answer: No. StrandsHint is an independent fan site. We provide hints and discussion to help players enjoy the puzzle. NYT Strands is a trademark of The New York Times Company.

faqItems.question: Can I browse past puzzle answers?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Our archive page has every past puzzle organized by date, with full answers, theme words, and the Spangram.

faqItems.question: What is the best strategy for solving Strands?
faqItems.answer: Start by reading the theme clue carefully — it hints at what the words have in common. Look for the Spangram first, as it spans the entire board and reveals the overarching theme. Then search for theme words by connecting adjacent letters horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.
